arts.ttl: Emmanuel Latte Lath scores a hat-trick as Middlesbrough hit Oxford United for six
arts.sum: Carrying on their fine pre-international break form, Finn Azaz also bagged a brace, with Tommy Conway off the bench to also grab one as Boro won 6-2 on what turned out to be a fine first visit to the Kassam Stadium. Coming into the game having been in fine goalscoring form before the break, the challenge for Boro was making sure the two-week break had not halted momentum.

arts.ttl: Michael Carrick's Dan Barlaser faith as Middlesbrough midfielder could get his big chance
arts.sum: Michael Carrick says he has 100 per cent faith in midfielder Dan Barlaser as injuries look set to hand the fringe man an opportunity the Middlesbrough side. Barlaser has struggled for regular game-time on Teesside since making the switch from Rotherham United almost two years ago.

arts.ttl: Mark Harris on his latest black eye, Wales and Craig Bellamy
arts.sum: MARK Harris is thoroughly enjoying his time away with Wales, even if he returned from the most recent camp with a second black eye in just over six months.

arts.ttl: Mark Harris on goal drought and why he won’t be chasing his next strike
arts.sum: MARK Harris is confident his goal drought will come to an end, having learnt from his experiences last season.

arts.ttl: Middlesbrough set Championship challenge as season enters Michael Carrick's favourite part
arts.sum: "I don’t think that it’s a crazy schedule really, but, for sure, it’s a much longer kind of chapter and more will get decided between now and March as by the time you come out of it you are not far from the end of the season. Boro will head into the game full of confidence, with many feeling their pre-break form was a sign that things had finally clicked for them after an inconsistent and at times frustrating start to the season.
International football is consigned to the memory bank for 2024 and there is a full slate of Championship action this weekend as Phil Agius previews all 12 games.

arts.ttl: Middlesbrough substitutes dilemma and 'massive strength' verdict for crucial moments
arts.sum: Michael Carrick has offered insight into his thinking when it comes to making substitutions in games - an aspect of his management at Middlesbrough that has at times come under scrutiny. “It’s never just about the 11 players on the pitch at the start of the game," Carrick said.

arts.ttl: ‘A tough place to go’ – Oxford United vs Middlesbrough prediction: The72
arts.sum: The72's writers offer their Oxford United vs Middlesbrough prediction ahead of the Championship clash on Saturday. The post ‘A tough place to go’ – Oxford United vs Middlesbrough prediction: The72 appeared first on The72 - Football League News.

arts.ttl: One tough Middlesbrough selection dilemma at Oxford with midfield injuries also a factor
arts.sum: With players returning from international duty and some injuries to contend with, Carrick will have some selection dilemmas for Oxford - with the fact it's the start of a three-game week no doubt a consideration too. With plenty of attacking options in his squad, Carrick could also consider dropping Riley McGree or Finn Azaz into the deeper role - with both having previously played as central midfielders.

arts.ttl: Middlesbrough's January transfer planning and Carrick's hint on potential squad manoeuvring
arts.sum: Boro are likely to have a relaxed stance to January's window - happy to monitor what options might crop up, without desperately chasing something, with no real major gaps in their squad as things stand that urgently need addressing. Middlesbrough are starting to plan for the January transfer window, with Michael Carrick offering hints that there could still be room to manoeuvre in their squad in order to create gaps for strengthening.
